verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the concrete first move: "Run this first"; B opens with framing: "almost always a symptom".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one first check and an order: "run the query above under load"; B surveys more branches before landing on the same idea.
verdict.fabrication: Both make unsupported frequency claims; A says "#1 cause" and B says "most common" plus "Past ~(2 × cores) + effective_spindles" without evidence.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on: one short query, clear state meanings, then two config checks; B is useful but heavier.
